UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an individual user within the ClientReview system, linked to an external authentication service. Stores application-specific user data, not authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically matching the user ID from the external authentication system.

displayName.description: The user's preferred name for display within the application.

profileImageUrl.description: An optional URL to the user's profile image.

role.description: The primary role of the user within the ClientReview system (e.g., 'AgencyAdmin', 'AgencyMember', 'ClientViewer').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Studio.title: Studio
Studio.description: Represents an agency, creative company, or construction company, serving as the root tenant for all projects, assets, and feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Studio entity.

name.description: The official name of the studio or agency.

description.description: A brief description of the studio.

ownerUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is the primary owner or creator of this studio. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Studio)

primaryColor.description: Hex color code for the studio's primary branding color (e.g., #455CA1).

backgroundColor.description: Hex color code for the studio's background branding color (e.g., #EBEDF9).

accentColor.description: Hex color code for the studio's accent branding color (e.g., #73BDF3).

headlineFont.description: The name of the font chosen for headlines (e.g., 'Space Grotesk').

bodyFont.description: The name of the font chosen for body text (e.g., 'Inter').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the studio was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the studio was last updated.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a specific client project managed by a studio, containing deliverables and feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

studioId.description: Reference to the Studio this project belongs to. (Relationship: Studio 1:N Project)

description.description: A detailed description of the project goals and scope.

status.description: The current status of the project (e.g., 'Draft', 'In Review', 'Approved', 'Archived').

clientAccessKey.description: A unique, secure key used to generate a gated URL for client access to this project. This is a token, not a password.

allowedClientUserIds.description: References to UserProfile IDs of clients who are authorized to access this specific project. (Relationship: Project N:N UserProfile)

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this project. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Project)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the project was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the project was last updated.

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Represents a high-resolution deliverable (e.g., image, video) uploaded for review within a project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this asset belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Asset)

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded asset.

fileUrl.description: Secure download URL for the asset stored in Firebase Cloud Storage.

thumbnailUrl.description: Optional URL for a smaller thumbnail image or video preview.

status.description: The current approval status of the asset (e.g., 'Pending Review', 'Approved').

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this asset. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Asset)

approvedByUserId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile who approved this asset, if applicable. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Asset)

approvedAt.description: Timestamp when the asset was approved, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the asset was uploaded.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the asset was last updated (e.g., status change).

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.description: Represents a specific time-coded or coordinate-based comment left by a client on an asset.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feedback entity.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset this feedback is related to. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Feedback)

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this feedback belongs to, primarily for query efficiency. (Relationship: Project 1:N Feedback)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (client) who left this feedback. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Feedback)

comment.description: The text content of the feedback comment.

timestampSeconds.description: Optional: For video assets, the exact timestamp in seconds where the feedback was left.

imageXCoordinate.description: Optional: For image assets, the X-coordinate (as a percentage, 0-1) where the feedback was left.

imageYCoordinate.description: Optional: For image assets, the Y-coordinate (as a percentage, 0-1) where the feedback was left.

isResolved.description: Indicates whether the agency has marked this feedback as addressed or resolved.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the feedback was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the feedback was last updated (e.g., resolved status changed).

definition.description: Stores application-specific user profiles, separate from authentication credentials. Access is typically self-owned, allowing users to manage their own profile data. Includes a 'role' field for global access distinctions (e.g., super admin).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, typically matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Represents a top-level agency or company acting as a tenant. Includes 'ownerUserId' for primary ownership and a 'members' map for collaborative roles within the studio, enabling granular access control for agency users. Also contains branding information for white-labeling.

params.name: studioId
params.description: The unique ID of the studio entity.

definition.description: Represents a specific project belonging to a studio. Includes denormalized 'studioOwnerId' and 'studioMembers' map (copied from the parent Studio) for authorization independence. The 'allowedClientUserIds' array specifies client access. Also includes 'createdByUserId'.

params.name: studioId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent studio.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the project.

definition.description: Represents a high-resolution deliverable (e.g., image, video) within a project. Includes denormalized 'studioId', 'studioOwnerId', 'studioMembers' map, and 'allowedClientUserIds' (copied from the parent Project) for authorization independence. Also includes 'uploadedByUserId'.

params.name: studioId
params.description: The unique ID of the grandparent studio.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent project.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique ID of the asset.

definition.description: Represents a time-coded or coordinate-based comment on an asset. Includes denormalized 'studioId', 'studioOwnerId', 'studioMembers' map, and 'allowedClientUserIds' (copied from the grandparent Project via Asset) for authorization independence. Also includes 'userId' for the client who left the feedback.

params.name: studioId
params.description: The unique ID of the great-grandparent studio.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique ID of the grandparent project.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent asset.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique ID of the feedback comment.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for the ClientReview application is designed following a multi-tenant, hierarchical model that prioritizes Authorization Independence, debuggability, and scalable security rules. The root of the tenancy is the 'Studio' collection, with 'Project', 'Asset', and 'Feedback' nested hierarchically within it.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This is the critical backbone of the design. To eliminate `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules and enable atomic operations, key authorization context from parent documents is denormalized into their child documents. Specifically:

1.  **Studio Membership:** The `Studio` entity is augmented with a `members` map (`{[uid]: 'role'}`), storing which agency users have what role within that specific studio. The `ownerUserId` is also present.
2.  **Project Denormalization:** Each `Project` document (residing in `/studios/{studioId}/projects/{projectId}`) explicitly includes:
    *   `studioOwnerId`: Copied from its parent `Studio`'s `ownerUserId`.
    *   `studioMembers`: A direct copy of its parent `Studio`'s `members` map.
    *   `allowedClientUserIds`: An array explicitly listing client UIDs with access to this project.
3.  **Asset Denormalization:** Each `Asset` document (in `/studios/{studioId}/projects/{projectId}/assets/{assetId}`) includes:
    *   `studioId`: The ID of the parent studio.
    *   `studioOwnerId`: Copied from its parent `Project`'s `studioOwnerId`.
    *   `studioMembers`: Copied from its parent `Project`'s `studioMembers` map.
    *   `allowedClientUserIds`: Copied from its parent `Project`'s `allowedClientUserIds`.
4.  **Feedback Denormalization:** Each `Feedback` document (in `/studios/{studioId}/projects/{projectId}/assets/{assetId}/feedback/{feedbackId}`) includes:
    *   `studioId`, `studioOwnerId`, `studioMembers`, `allowedClientUserIds`: All copied from its parent `Asset`.

This extensive denormalization means that any access rule for a `Project`, `Asset`, or `Feedback` document can be evaluated solely by inspecting the `request.auth.uid` against the `resource.data` of the document itself, without needing to perform a `get()` on any parent document. This greatly simplifies rule writing, debugging, and prevents issues with transactional atomicity.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters) Support:**
The structural segregation and denormalization strategy directly enable secure and efficient `list` operations:

*   **UserProfile:** `/user_profiles/{userId}` allows self-read/write. Global admin roles (e.g., via a separate `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection) can query all profiles.
*   **Studio:** A user can query for studios where `request.auth.uid == studio.ownerUserId` or `studio.members[request.auth.uid]` exists. This allows listing only studios the current user is associated with, without rules filtering query results.
*   **Project, Asset, Feedback:** Given their nested paths, queries can efficiently target specific studios or projects (e.g., `collection('studios/{studioId}/projects').where('studioId', '==', studioId)`). The security rules will then enforce that the `request.auth.uid` is either a `studioOwnerId`, a key in `studioMembers`, or one of the `allowedClientUserIds` within the `resource.data` of the documents being accessed. This ensures that only authorized users can list relevant documents, fulfilling the QAP principle.

**Overall Structure and Access Modeling:**

*   **`/user_profiles/{userId}`:** Private data, owner-based access (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
*   **`/studios/{studioId}`:** Collaborative data, with `ownerUserId` and a `members` map defining roles for agency users. This acts as the tenant root.
*   **`/studios/{studioId}/projects/{projectId}`:** Hierarchical path for studio-owned projects. Access for agency users is determined by denormalized `studioOwnerId` or `studioMembers`. Client access is determined by `allowedClientUserIds` array. The `Project` schema includes `createdByUserId` for more granular internal permissions.
*   **`/studios/{studioId}/projects/{projectId}/assets/{assetId}`:** Continued hierarchy for project deliverables. Access mirrors that of the parent `Project` via denormalized fields. The `Asset` schema includes `uploadedByUserId` for specific asset ownership/management.
*   **`/studios/{studioId}/projects/{projectId}/assets/{assetId}/feedback/{feedbackId}`:** Deeply nested for specific feedback comments. Access is governed by the inherited denormalized fields. The `Feedback` schema includes `userId` for the client who provided the feedback, enabling self-moderation of comments.

This design leverages structural segregation, consistent access modeling, and radical denormalization to create a secure, scalable, and highly debuggable Firestore schema for ClientReview.
